## [title-slide] Welcome to AI-Native Dev

![Title](images/slide-01-title.png)

<!-- AUDIO -->
[HOOK] What if writing code felt less like fighting with syntax and more like having a conversation with a brilliant collaborator?

Welcome everyone. I'm Jane Developer, and today we're going to explore how AI is fundamentally changing the way we build software.

[PAUSE:500]

This isn't about AI replacing developers. It's about AI amplifying what developers can do.
<!-- /AUDIO -->

**Speaker Notes:**
- Make eye contact with the audience
- Gauge the room's technical level
- Pause for effect after the hook

---

## [hook-problem] The Current State

![The Problem](images/slide-02-hook.png)

<!-- AUDIO -->
[TRANSITION] But first, let's talk about where we are today.

The software development landscape is facing unprecedented challenges.

[KEY_POINT] Development cycles are getting shorter while complexity is increasing exponentially.

Teams are expected to deliver more features, faster, with fewer bugs. And the tools we've been using for decades are struggling to keep up.

[PAUSE:300]

Sound familiar?
<!-- /AUDIO -->

**Speaker Notes:**
- Rhetorical question to engage audience
- Let the statistics on the next slide speak for themselves

---

## [evidence-stats] By the Numbers

![Statistics](images/slide-03-stats.png)

<!-- AUDIO -->
[EVIDENCE] Let me share some data that might surprise you.

According to recent studies, developers spend only 30% of their time actually writing new code. The rest goes to debugging, reading documentation, and context switching.

[KEY_POINT] That's 70% of our working hours on tasks that don't directly add value.

[PAUSE:400]

Imagine if we could reclaim even half of that time.
<!-- /AUDIO -->

**Speaker Notes:**
- Source: Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2025
- GitHub State of Octoverse report
- These numbers resonate with most developers

---

## [solution-intro] A New Approach

![Solution](images/slide-04-solution.png)

<!-- AUDIO -->
[TRANSITION] Now let's talk about how AI-native development changes everything.

[KEY_POINT] AI-native development isn't about bolting AI onto existing workflows. It's about reimagining the development process from the ground up.

When AI understands your codebase, your intent, and your patterns, it becomes a true force multiplier.

[STORY] One team I worked with reduced their time-to-production by 40% after adopting AI-native practices.

They didn't work longer hours. They worked smarter.
<!-- /AUDIO -->

**Speaker Notes:**
- Emphasize the word "reimagining"
- The story is from Acme Corp (anonymized)

---

## [demo-walkthrough] See It In Action

![Demo](images/slide-05-demo.png)

<!-- AUDIO -->
Let me show you what this looks like in practice.

[PAUSE:300]

Watch as I describe what I want to build, and the AI collaborates with me in real-time.

[KEY_POINT] Notice how I'm not fighting with syntax. I'm describing intent.

The AI handles the boilerplate while I focus on the logic that matters.

[CALLBACK] Remember that 70% of time we talked about earlier? This is how we reclaim it.
<!-- /AUDIO -->

**Speaker Notes:**
- Live demo section - have backup video ready
- Highlight the natural language interface
- Show error correction in action

---

## [call-to-action] Get Started Today

![Call to Action](images/slide-06-cta.png)

<!-- AUDIO -->
[LANDING] So where do we go from here?

[CTA] I want you to try one thing this week. Take your most tedious coding task and see if an AI assistant can help.

Start small. Build confidence. Then expand.

[PAUSE:500]

The future of software development isn't about AI versus humans.

It's about what humans can accomplish when AI has their back.

Thank you.
<!-- /AUDIO -->

**Speaker Notes:**
- Strong close - make eye contact
- Leave time for questions
- Have QR code ready for resources
